rows.wrapperRole: Wrapper varies, so it teaches how Natural, Maduro, or Cameroon wrappers can change the same Fuente core.
rows.binderRole: Dominican binder supports Fuente's smooth construction and balanced burn.
rows.fillerRole: Dominican filler provides classic cedar, mild spice, and approachable premium structure.
rows.flavorNotes: cedar, baking spice, nuts, light sweetness, toast
rows.avoidIf: User needs a single fixed flavor profile or wants strong nicotine intensity.
rows.whyRecommend: Recommend as a classic Dominican premium that teaches construction and balance.
rows.catalogLine: Arturo Fuente Gran Reserva Rothschild · 4½ × 50 · Rothschild · Medium-Light · Dominican · $7 · Notes: cedar, baking spice, wood, light sweetness
rows.priceNote: Wrapper varies (Natural/Maduro/Cameroon); MSRP for Natural Rothschild.
rows.sizeNote: Line also: Chateau, Cazadores — length/ring vary.

rows.wrapperRole: Connecticut Shade wrapper softens the profile with cream, light cedar, and a clean mild finish.
rows.binderRole: Mexican binder adds structure and a quiet savory base without making the cigar aggressive.
rows.fillerRole: Dominican and Mexican filler keep the smoke mild, consistent, and easy to read.
rows.flavorNotes: vanilla, bread, sweet spice, coffee, cinnamon, nutmeg
rows.avoidIf: User wants bold pepper, heavy body, high nicotine, or collector rarity.
rows.whyRecommend: Recommend when the user needs a gentle, reliable, beginner-safe Connecticut profile.
rows.catalogLine: Macanudo Cafe Hyde Park · 5½ × 50 · Robusto · Light · Dominican · $8 · Notes: cream, cedar, light nuts, toast

rows.displayName: Montecristo White Series Toro / Rothchilde / Churchill
rows.wrapperRole: Ecuadorian Connecticut Shade wrapper delivers cream, smoothness, and light sweetness.
rows.binderRole: Nicaraguan binder adds savory structure and keeps the mild profile from feeling thin.
rows.fillerRole: Dominican and Nicaraguan long-fillers provide a creamy core with gentle spice and enough body for pairing.
rows.flavorNotes: cream, cinnamon spice, wood, leather, toasty
rows.avoidIf: User wants strong pepper, dark flavors, or short smoke.
rows.whyRecommend: Recommend for a smooth, creamy premium cigar where the binder adds quiet savory depth.
rows.catalogLine: Montecristo White Series Toro · 6 × 54 · Toro · Medium-Light · Dominican · $11 · Notes: cream, cedar, light spice, toast

rows.displayName: Romeo y Julieta 1875 Churchill / Bully / Toro
rows.wrapperRole: Indonesian Shade wrapper brings approachable earth, toast, and a soft spice edge.
rows.binderRole: Dominican binder keeps the blend steady and traditional rather than sharp.
rows.fillerRole: Dominican filler gives cedar, earth, nuts, and mild-to-medium Cuban-heritage character.
rows.avoidIf: User dislikes earthy profiles or wants creamy Connecticut sweetness.
rows.whyRecommend: Recommend when the user wants a classic, familiar Dominican profile with more character than ultra-mild cigars.
rows.catalogLine: Romeo y Julieta 1875 Churchill · 7 × 47 · Churchill · Medium · Dominican · $9 · Notes: cedar, earth, toasted nuts, light pepper

rows.displayName: La Flor Dominicana Double Ligero Chisel / Robusto / Toro
rows.wrapperRole: Ecuadorian Sumatra wrapper (natural or maduro) frames the ligero core with spice, earth, and oily depth without softening the strength.
rows.binderRole: Dominican binder supports combustion and structure through a dense double-ligero bunch.
rows.fillerRole: Dominican ligero filler drives exceptional nicotine, red pepper, leather, coffee, and experienced-only intensity.
rows.flavorNotes: Red pepper, leather, charred oak, earth, coffee, dark spice, mineral tobacco.
rows.bestFor: Experienced smoker, after dinner, rum pairing, user wants a Dominican power lesson—not the mild DR stereotype.
rows.avoidIf: Beginner, nicotine-sensitive, empty stomach, morning coffee, user wants creamy Connecticut mildness.
rows.whyRecommend: Recommend when the user seeks maximum Dominican ligero intensity and understands this is among the strongest profiles in the book.
rows.catalogLine: La Flor Dominicana Double Ligero Chisel · 6 × 54 · Chisel · Full · Dominican · $13 · Notes: red pepper, leather, charred oak, earth

rows.displayName: My Father Le Bijou 1922 Toro / Torpedo / Churchill
rows.wrapperRole: Nicaraguan Habano Oscuro wrapper deepens cocoa, leather, and pepper with oily maduro texture.
rows.binderRole: Nicaraguan binder (often double-binder bunch) amplifies Garcia-family structure and full-bodied burn.
rows.fillerRole: Nicaraguan filler drives espresso, dark cocoa, earth, cherry-dark fruit, and experienced-only intensity.
rows.flavorNotes: Dark cocoa, espresso, leather, black pepper, earth, dark fruit, charred wood.
rows.bestFor: Experienced smoker, winter smoke, after-dinner Scotch, user wants dark Nicaraguan Garcia body.
rows.avoidIf: Beginner, mild preference, morning smoke, user dislikes pepper and heavy body.
rows.whyRecommend: Recommend for a serious, dark, full-bodied Nicaraguan profile with My Father blending identity.
rows.catalogLine: My Father Le Bijou 1922 Toro · 6 × 52 · Toro · Full · Nicaragua · $13 · Notes: dark cocoa, espresso, leather, black pepper

rows.displayName: Oliva Serie V Double Robusto / Torpedo / Toro
rows.wrapperRole: Ecuadorian Habano Sun Grown wrapper adds oily sweetness, spice, and Jalapa/Estelí character on the nose.
rows.binderRole: Nicaraguan binder supports full body and ligero-forward structure.
rows.fillerRole: Nicaraguan ligero filler delivers espresso, pepper, dark cocoa, and long-finishing strength.
rows.flavorNotes: Sweet spice, espresso, dark cocoa, cedar, leather, black pepper, earth.
rows.bestFor: Experienced smoker, after dinner, bourbon pairing, user wants full Nicaraguan intensity with sweetness.
rows.avoidIf: Beginner, empty stomach, short time window, user avoids high nicotine.
rows.whyRecommend: Recommend when the user asks for rich Nicaraguan power with sweetness and structure.
rows.catalogLine: Oliva Serie V Double Robusto · 6 × 54 · Double Robusto · Full · Nicaragua · $11 · Notes: sweet spice, espresso, dark cocoa, cedar

rows.notes: Padron portfolio — Entry tier (Thousands) — 2000 Robusto natural — aged 2.5–3 years — crisp wood, earth, cocoa, nuts. Natural wrapper sibling to CUR-43 maduro.
rows.wrapperRole: Nicaraguan Habano natural or maduro wrapper sets cocoa/coffee tone and teaches wrapper-driven sweetness vs depth.
rows.binderRole: Nicaraguan binder reinforces puro identity and rustic, honest Padron construction.
rows.fillerRole: Nicaraguan filler drives cocoa, earth, coffee, and dependable medium body—the classic daily Padron profile.
rows.flavorNotes: Cocoa, coffee, earth, nuts, cedar, light pepper, sweet tobacco.
rows.bestFor: Quick lunch break, coffee pairing, Nicaraguan baseline lesson, value-conscious premium smoker.
rows.avoidIf: Beginner nicotine-sensitive, user wants Dominican cream or anniversary-line collector rarity.
rows.whyRecommend: Recommend as the reference Nicaraguan puro for balanced body without 1964/1926 pricing.
rows.memberBlurb: The daily Padron lesson — all-Nicaraguan cocoa, coffee, and earth in honest construction. Natural vs maduro is your sweetness dial.
